rationale.fidelity: Accurately distinguishes an executable feasible incumbent from a certificate conditional on bound-gap closure and preserves the scheduling model.
rationale.managerial_framing: Explains the different managerial roles of schedule quality and proof, though approval and risk pathways are appropriately only hypothetical.
rationale.scholarly_positioning: Bounds the analytical distinction but offers no literature positioning.
rationale.evidence_discipline: Calls deadline certification unsupported and requests deadline-specific runtime evidence before any promise.
rationale.overall: A strong explanation of feasibility versus optimality that avoids promising certification under short deadlines.
